The application is an educational platform.
The application is a gamified competency assessment platform.
Instructors can register on the platform.
Instructors can log in on the platform.
Instructors can manage a personal dashboard.
The personal dashboard shows active exams.
The personal dashboard shows enrollment counts.
The personal dashboard shows competency coverage.
Instructors can build exams with mixed question types.
The mixed question types include MCQ.
The mixed question types include drag-and-drop.
The mixed question types include code snippet.
Each question can be tagged with one or more competencies.
Each question can be assigned a difficulty tier.
The difficulty tiers are Beginner.
The difficulty tiers are Intermediate.
The difficulty tiers are Advanced.
The difficulty tier determines a nugget reward multiplier for the question.
Instructors can schedule exams with an open date.
Instructors can schedule exams with a close date.
Instructors can schedule exams with an optional per-attempt time limit.
The per-attempt time limit is enforced server-side.
Instructors can create cohorts.
Instructors can enroll students individually into cohorts.
Instructors can restrict each exam to one or more cohorts.
Instructors can view a per-competency heatmap after each exam.
The heatmap highlights competencies below a configurable mastery threshold in red.
Instructors can grant manual bonus nuggets to individual students.
The manual bonus nugget grant requires a written justification.
The written justification is logged as an audit event.
Students can register on the platform.
Students can personalize a profile with an avatar.
The student profile displays a nugget balance.
The student profile displays earned badges.
The student profile displays a competency radar chart.
Exams are delivered to students one question at a time.
The exam delivery interface is game-like.
The exam delivery interface includes a live timer.
The exam delivery interface includes a progress bar.
The exam delivery interface includes an animated nugget counter.
The platform has a streak system.
The streak system applies a nugget multiplier for consecutive correct answers.
The nugget multiplier can be 1.5×.
The nugget multiplier can be 2×.
The nugget multiplier can be 3×.
The nugget multiplier resets on any wrong answer.
Students are shown an instant competency breakdown after submission.
The competency breakdown flags weak competencies.
The competency breakdown provides a study tip for each flagged weak competency.
Each student has a nugget wallet.
The nugget wallet is redeemable in a reward store.
The reward store offers virtual items.
The reward store offers instructor-configured institutional perks.
Students are ranked on a cohort leaderboard.
The leaderboard ranking is by nuggets earned over a configurable window.
Students can opt out of the leaderboard.
Instructors can disable the leaderboard.
Students can review every past attempt.
The past attempt review shows the student's answers.
The past attempt review shows the correct answers.
The past attempt review shows nuggets earned per question.
The past attempt review shows competency trend charts over time.
The platform enforces role-based access control.
The roles are Admin.
The roles are Instructor.
The roles are Student.
Every sensitive action is written to an immutable audit log.
Instructors can create accounts and log in to the system to access their personal dashboard.
The dashboard displays the number of active exams, total enrolled students, and how many competencies are covered.
Users with instructor privileges authenticate through a secure login mechanism to reach their management interface.
Upon login, an instructor sees a summary including ongoing assessments, participant counts, and competency coverage percentages.
A teacher registers, signs in, and views a control panel showing live tests, enrollment figures, and skill coverage.
Using the exam builder, instructors compose assessments from multiple-choice questions, drag-and-drop items, and code snippet exercises.
Each question in the exam can be associated with multiple competencies and assigned a difficulty level of Beginner, Intermediate, or Advanced.
The difficulty tier attached to a question determines the nugget multiplier that students will earn for answering correctly.
Instructors tag questions with one or more skill areas and set their difficulty to control the reward rate.
This tool lets teachers create tests with various formats, each linked to competencies and a difficulty-based nugget bonus.
Exams are scheduled by setting an opening date, a closing date, and optionally a time limit per attempt that is enforced on the server side.
The per-attempt time limit is controlled centrally by the server to ensure consistency across all students.
Instructors define availability windows for assessments and may impose a strict duration for each student's session.
The scheduled assessment becomes accessible only after the open date and expires after the close date, with server-side time enforcement.
Teachers configure exam timing by specifying start and end dates and can also add a maximum duration for each attempt, managed by the backend.
Instructors can form cohorts, enroll individual students into them, and restrict exam access to one or more specified cohorts.
An exam can be limited so only students belonging to particular cohorts are allowed to take it.
Cohort creation allows instructors to group learners and then assign exams exclusively to those groups.
Teachers assemble groups of students and then associate exams with those groups to control who participates.
This feature enables the restriction of assessments to specific student groups that the instructor has created and enrolled.
Educators are able to award extra nuggets manually to a particular student, and they must provide a written reason that gets recorded as an audit event.
Manual bonus nuggets given to individual learners require a justification note that is stored in an immutable audit log.
Instructors can grant additional nuggets to a student, with a mandatory explanation logged for accountability.
Teachers can issue bonus rewards to a specific student, and every such action leaves an audit trail with the stated rationale.
Extra nuggets can be assigned to a student by an instructor, accompanied by a text justification that becomes part of the audit history.
Students can create accounts and customize their profiles by choosing an avatar, and their dashboard displays nugget balance, earned badges, and a competency radar chart.
New learners sign up and personalize their profile with a picture, then see their total nuggets, achievement badges, and a visual radar of their skills.
The student's home screen shows a personalized avatar, current nugget count, all badges they have earned, and a radar chart of their competencies.
After registration, learners set their profile image and can view their nuggets, badges collected, and a radar graph representing their competency strengths.
Learner profiles are customizable with an avatar, and the interface presents the nugget wallet, badge collection, and a radar visualization of competency levels.
During an exam, questions appear one at a time in a game-like interface featuring a live countdown timer, a progress bar, and an animated nugget counter.
The assessment is delivered sequentially per question in a playful environment with a real-time timer, a visual progress indicator, and a moving nugget counter.
Each question is shown individually in an interactive, game-style presentation that includes a running clock, a bar showing progress, and a dynamic nugget score display.
Exams present questions singly in an engaging interface with a live timer, a visual progress tracker, and an animated display of nuggets earned so far.
The testing experience shows one question per screen in a gamified layout, equipped with a real-time timer, a progress bar, and a nugget tally that updates with animation.
The streak system awards a nugget multiplier of 1.5x, 2x, or 3x for consecutive correct answers, and the multiplier resets to 1x upon any incorrect answer.
Correct answers in a row trigger an increasing multiplier on nugget rewards, and a wrong answer drops the multiplier back to the base value.
Consecutive correct responses apply a progression of reward multipliers (1.5, 2, then 3 times) which vanish if the student answers incorrectly.
A chain of correct answers builds up a multiplier that boosts nugget earnings, but any mistake resets it completely.
Getting several questions right in succession increases the nugget multiplier through three stages, and a single incorrect answer eliminates the bonus.
Immediately after submitting the exam, students see a competency breakdown that highlights weak areas and provides a study tip for each flagged competency.
A post-submission summary shows which competencies were weak, marks them clearly, and gives a specific study suggestion for each.
Upon completion, the system displays an instant analysis of the student's competencies, pointing out deficiencies with advice on how to improve.
Right after the test finishes, learners receive a per-competency evaluation with weaknesses emphasized and accompanied by recommended study actions.
The final submission triggers an immediate competency assessment that flags low performance areas and offers a remedial tip for each flagged skill.
Students have a nugget wallet whose balance can be spent in a reward store on virtual items or institution-configured perks set by instructors.
The learner's nugget account is redeemable in a shop for digital goods or for real-world benefits that instructors have configured.
Nuggets accumulated by a student are stored in a wallet and can be used to purchase virtual rewards or instructor-defined institutional privileges from a store.
A student's nugget balance functions as currency in a rewards marketplace, exchangeable for virtual products or perks arranged by the instructor.
The reward store lets students use their nugget wallet to acquire virtual items or institution-specific benefits that the instructor has set up.
A cohort leaderboard ranks students by total nuggets earned over a configurable time period, with options for students to opt out or for instructors to disable it entirely.
Students are positioned on a leaderboard within their cohort based on nuggets collected during a customizable window, and participation can be turned off by the student or the teacher.
The leaderboard shows cohort members ordered by nugget earnings for a chosen timeframe, and both students (via opt-out) and instructors (via disable) can hide rankings.
A ranked list of students in a cohort is created from nugget totals over an adjustable period, and individuals can withdraw or the instructor can deactivate the board.
Students see where they stand among cohort peers in nuggets earned during a settable period, and there are controls to opt out or for the teacher to disable the leaderboard.
Students can review every past attempt, seeing their own answers, the correct answers, nuggets earned per question, and competency trend charts over time.
Past test attempts are available for review, showing the student's submitted answers alongside the correct ones, per-question nugget earnings, and charts tracking competency changes.
Learners can revisit any completed exam to compare their answers with the correct answers, view nuggets for each item, and examine charts of competency development.
Every previous attempt is accessible, displaying what the student answered, the correct responses, nuggets awarded per question, and graphs of competency progression.
Students have access to a history of their attempts that includes their input, the correct choices, per-question nugget gains, and trend graphs for each competency.
